def: A combining form (also used adjectively), designating any oneof a series of double derivatives of the azo and diazo compoundscontaining four atoms of nitrogen.
Measurement of the heart, as by percussion or auscultation.

Y, the twenty-fifth letter of the English alphabet, at thebeginning of a word or syllable, except when a prefix (see Y-), isusually a fricative vocal consonant; as a prefix, and usually in themiddle or at the end of a syllable, it is a vowel. See Guide toPronunciation, §§ 145, 178-9, 272.
Tending to mitigate; mitigating; lentitive. Johnson.
The amphitheater of Vespasian at Rome, the largest in theworld. [Written also Colosseum.]
A sonneter, or sonneteer. Bp. Hall.
